title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
overview: A century before the events of Game of Thrones, two unlikely heroes wandered Westeros: a young, naive but courageous knight, Ser Duncan the Tall, and his diminutive squire, Egg. Set in an age when the Targaryen line still holds the Iron Throne and the last dragon has not yet passed from living memory, great destinies, powerful foes, and dangerous exploits await these improbable and incomparable friends.

title: Spartacus: House of Ashur
overview: In a world where he survived the events of Spartacus, Ashur clawed his way to power, owning the same ludus that once owned him. Allying with a fierce gladiatrix, Ashur ignites a new kind of spectacle that offends the elite.

title: Pluribus
overview: The most miserable person on Earth must save the world from happiness.
reason: Critically acclaimed with an outstanding 8.0 IMDb rating from over 127,000 votes and a high Metacritic score of 87. It's helmed by renowned creator Vince Gilligan and features Emmy-nominated Rhea Seehorn on Apple TV.

title: Wonder Man
overview: Simon and Trevor, two actors at opposite ends of their careers, chase life-changing roles.
reason: Leveraging the massive appeal of Marvel Studios and Disney+, this series has a strong 7.6 IMDb rating. Its connection to the MCU ensures significant cultural relevance and viewership.

title: The Beast in Me
overview: A famous author is pulled into a twisted mind game with her rich, powerful new neighbor — who might be a murderer.
reason: This Netflix drama boasts a solid 7.4 IMDb rating from over 61,000 votes, indicating strong audience engagement. Its appeal is further enhanced by acclaimed lead actors Claire Danes and Matthew Rhys.

title: Man vs Baby
overview: As Christmas approaches, a blundering-all-the-way dad juggles housesitting a posh London penthouse with an unexpected pickle: caring for a lost baby.
